name: Czech B2 Vocabulary
description: Upper-intermediate Czech — CEFR B2 level vocabulary

facts.explanation: taška — bag (flexible container). Also: roof tile
trip — LSD. Also: period of time in which one experiences drug-induced reverie or hallucinations
tyč — rod (a straight, round stick, shaft, bar, cane, or staff).
žid — Jew (by religion). Also: miser, scrooge
Alpy — Alps (a mountain range in Western Europe).

Asie — Asia (the largest continent, located between Europe and the Pacific Ocean). Also: Asia (an ancient province of the Roman Empire in western Anatolia, existing between 133 BC (during the Roman Republic) and the 7th century AD)
bomba — bomb (explosive). Also: howitzer (hard shot), smash hit, gas cylinder
brána — gate. Also: goal (an area into which the players attempt to put an object; the term branka is more commonly used in this sense), gateway
buňka — cell (basic unit of a living organism).
cedule — sign (a clearly visible object, generally flat, bearing a short message in words or pictures).
čára — line. Also: threadlike mark of pen, pencil, or graver
čerpadlo — pump (device for moving liquid or gas).
čip — chip (circuit fabricated in one piece on a small, thin substrate).
dáma — lady. Also: checkers (US), draughts (UK), a component of certain games, queen, king
Dán — Dane (person from Denmark or of Danish descent).
dědic — heir (of property). Also: heir (of title or office)
dieta — diet (a controlled regimen of food and drink, as to gain or lose weight or otherwise influence health).
díra — hole (hollow in some surface). Also: hole (opening in a solid), hole (undesirable place to live)
dohled — view (range of vision). Also: supervision
družstvo — team. Also: squad, cooperative (type of company that is owned partially or wholly by its employees, customers or tenants)
flek — spot. Also: job, employment, post, a kind of small pasta, raise, double (multiplies the current stake by 2)
hodnost — rank (a level in an organization such as the military).
holý — bald; hairless. Also: bare, naked
hřeben — comb (for hair). Also: ridge (of hills)
chudý — poor (having little or no possessions and money).
ikona — icon. Also: An image, symbol, or pictogram; an icon
Irák — Iraq (a country in West Asia in the Middle East).
Írán — Iran (a country in West Asia in the Middle East).
jídelní lístek — menu (printed list of dishes).
kachna — duck (bird). Also: female duck, an untrue, made-up message (similar to hoax, excuse), an incompetent, dull woman
klidný — calm (of a person). Also: calm (of a place)
kodex — code (set of principles or rules).
kost — bone (any of the components of an endoskeleton, made of bone). Also: girl, woman
koza — goat. Also: tit (woman's breast), sawhorse, time trial bicycle
kozí — goat. Also: goatlike, similar to something of a goat
kruh — disc (circular plate). Also: wheel, circle (group of associated people)
křídlo — wing. Also: grand piano, outside
ksicht — face (the front part of the head).
kšeft — deal (instance of buying or selling). Also: shop
Kuba — Cuba (a country, the largest island (based on land area) in the Caribbean).
kůra — bark (of a tree). Also: crust (of the Earth or of bread), cortex, rind (of fruits such as orange and lemon)
Kyjev — Kyiv (the capital city of Ukraine).
len — flax (any plant of the genus Linum). Also: flax (fibers)
mýto — toll (fee, especially of roads and bridges).
nábřeží — bank (edge of river, lake, or other watercourse).
nájemné — rent (payment for an apartment or other equipment).
nálada — mood (mental or emotional state, composure). Also: mood (disposition to do something)
oblek — suit (set of clothes to be worn together).
obrat — turn (change of direction or orientation). Also: backflip, U-turn (change in policy or opinion), turning point, phrase (short written or spoken expression), turnover
oheň — fire. Also: conflagration, fire; shooting, fever
okruh — ring. Also: beltway, orbital motorway, ring road
osa — axis (of symmetry or rotation). Also: axis (of a coordinate system)
past — trap (a device designed to catch and sometimes kill animals).
pětka — five (digit or figure). Also: ten korunas, try (type of scoring play worth five points)
podoba — form. Also: semblance, appearance, look
pořadatel — host (person or organisation responsible for running an event).
použitý — used. Also: used (that has or have previously been owned by someone else)
pozdní — late (at the end of a period).
příze — yarn (fiber strand for knitting or weaving).
půlka — half (one of two equal parts of something). Also: cheek (buttock)
reálný — real (not imaginary or fabricated). Also: real, real (not nominal; adjusted for inflation)

roh — horn (a hard growth of keratin that protrudes from the top of the head of certain animals). Also: horn (any of several musical wind instruments), corner (of a street), corner (the space in the angle between converging walls which meet in a point)
rovný — even (flat and level). Also: equal
ryba — fish (cold-blooded vertebrate animal that lives in water, moving with the help of fins and breathing with gills).

Řím — Rome (a major city, the capital of Italy and the Italian region of Lazio, located on the Tiber River; the ancient capital of the Roman Empire). Also: Rome (a metropolitan city of Lazio, Italy), Rome, Ancient Rome (an ancient empire based out of the city of Rome, covering vast territories in Europe, Asia and Africa; in full, Roman Empire)
sál — room. Also: saloon, hall, theater (operating theater for surgery)
sedadlo — seat (a place in which to sit).
skála — rock (mass of stone projecting out of the ground or water).
sláva — fame (state of being well-known and spoken of). Also: glory
srst — hair (the mass of filaments growing from the skin of animals, not humans).
SSSR — USSR (a former transcontinental country in Europe and Asia (1922–1991), now split into Russia and 14 other countries; the Soviet Union; in full, Svaz sovětských socialistických republik).
stezka — path (a trail for the use of, or worn by, pedestrians).
strava — diet (food a person or animal consumes). Also: board, meals (regular meals)

sůl — salt (A common substance, chemically consisting mainly of sodium chloride (NaCl), used extensively as a condiment and preservative.). Also: salt (One of the compounds formed from the reaction of an acid with a base, where a positive ion replaces a hydrogen of the acid.), salt (Additional bytes inserted into a plaintext message before encryption, in order to increase randomness and render brute-force decryption more difficult.)
svobodný — free (not imprisoned or enslaved). Also: single (not married), free, libre (with very few limitations on distribution or improvement)

tah — pull (act of pulling). Also: move, step (in accomplishing a plan), stroke (a line made with a brush), move, turn (act of moving a token on a gameboard from one position to another according to the rules of the game)
těleso — body (physical object). Also: division ring, ensemble, group (of art)
tvrdý — hard (resistant to pressure). Also: difficult, hard (demanding a lot of effort to endure)
úloha — task (a piece of work done as part of one’s duties).
úsvit — dawn (the time when the sun rises).
uzel — knot (looping). Also: knot (nautical unit of speed), node (vertex or a leaf in a graph of a network)
vazba — bond. Also: bond (a specific pattern of bricklaying), coupling, custody, pin
vedro — heat (condition or quality of being hot).
víla — vila (Slavic mythology). Also: fairy (mythical being)
vrána — crow (bird of the genus Corvus).
zobák — beak (in birds). Also: mouth (human mouth)
zóna — zone (area distinguished on the basis of a particular characteristic).
zpravodajství — news. Also: reporting, intelligence (information, often secret, about enemies, rivals, hostile activities, etc.)
zvon — bell (metallic resonating object). Also: plunger, plumber's friend
železo — iron (chemical element). Also: iron (a type of golf club)
Andy — Andes (a mountain range in South America).
apartmá — suite (group of connected rooms, usually separable from other rooms by means of access).
cibule — onion (Allium cepa). Also: bulb (bulb-shaped root), turnip (old-fashioned pocket watch)
cyklus — cycle. Also: series, loop (sequence of instructions repeated until or while a condition is satisfied)
čepel — blade (sharp-edged or pointed working end of a tool or utensil).
černoch — black (person of African descent, of dark complexion).
dietní — light (low in fat, calories, alcohol, salt, etc.).
Dillí — Delhi (a megacity and union territory of India, containing the national capital New Delhi).
dráha — track (for a race or exercise). Also: lane (a division of a racetrack), trajectory, railway (a transport system), railroad (the transportation system)
drsný — rough (having a texture that has much friction; not smooth). Also: harsh
graf — graph (mathematical diagram). Also: graph (nodes and edges connecting the nodes), chart (graphical presentation)
hledí — visor. Also: the part of the sights of a gun closer to the eye when aiming
hluk — noise (usually unwanted mixture of sounds).
hrdost — pride (sense of one's own worth, and abhorrence of what is beneath or unworthy of one).
husa — goose. Also: female goose, stupid woman (objectionable woman)
hustý — dense. Also: viscous, good, cool
Japonsko — Japan (a country and archipelago of East Asia).
jeřáb — rowan (a tree of the genus Sorbus). Also: crane (mechanical lifting device)
jevy — again. Also: on the other hand
kalich — calyx. Also: major calyx, chalice, goblet, cup; something that is to be endured or received
kanalizace — sewer (pipes used to remove human waste and to provide drainage).
kari — curry (mixture of ground spices). Also: curry (dish)
kmen — trunk (of a tree). Also: log (the trunk of a dead tree, cleared of branches), tribe (group of people), phylum, strain (variant of species, like of virus), stem
kobra — cobra (various venomous snakes of the family Elapidae). Also: cobra (a method of playing the ball using stiff fingers above the head)
konat — to do. Also: to take place
koruna — crown (royal, imperial, or princely headdress). Also: crown, monarchy, koruna (Czech currency), coin or banknote with the value of one Czech koruna, crown, corolla, fermata
kouzla — magic (supernatural method to dominate natural forces).
královský — royal (of or relating to a monarch or their family).
kříž — cross. Also: crucifix, club (playing card symbol), sacrum
kurt — court (place arranged for playing the games of tennis, basketball, squash, badminton, volleyball and some other games).
legální — legal (allowed or prescribed by law).
lehký — light (of low weight). Also: easy
Libye — Libya (a country in North Africa).
loket — elbow (joint). Also: ell, cubit (unit of length)
med — honey (thick, viscous, sweet liquid made by bees).
mez — limit. Also: balk (narrow strip of uncultivated land between cultivated fields)

Milán — Milan (a city and comune, the capital of the Metropolitan City of Milan and the region of Lombardy, Italy). Also: Milan (a metropolitan city of Lombardy, established in 2015; in full, the Metropolitan City of Milan)
milost — mercy. Also: grace, pardon, grace (A title for a member of nobility or a king)
milovník — lover (a person who loves something). Also: lover (the character of a lover or an actor for such roles)
morální — moral (of or relating to principles of right and wrong). Also: moral (conforming to a standard of right behaviour)
mráz — frost (cold weather able to freeze). Also: frost (cover of minute ice crystals)
náčelník — chief (leader of a tribe). Also: chief (head of an organization)
námořní — naval (of or relating to a navy). Also: nautical
napřed — first (of all). Also: in advance, ahead
nevlastní — step-; related through marriage or adoption rather than through a common ancestor.
objednávka — order (request for some product or service).
obor hodnot — range (the set of values which a function can obtain).
oddíl — troop (of scout girls and boys). Also: partition (of a hard drive), section (of a document)
odstín — shade (variety of color). Also: shade (subtle variation in a concept)
omáčka — sauce (liquid condiment). Also: padding (superfluous sentences added to a speech or text to make it longer)
orel — eagle (bird). Also: tail (side of a coin)
orgán — organ (part of an organism). Also: authority, body (functional part of a government or an organization; organized group of people)
ostrý — sharp (able to cut easily). Also: hot (of food and spice), strict (of an inequality, such that it rules out the possibility of equality), acute (of an angle, having less than 90 degrees)
palec — thumb. Also: big toe, inch
Paříž — Paris (the capital and largest city of France).
perla — pearl (rounded shelly concretion produced by certain mollusks).
pochod — march (way of walking). Also: march (song), process
posun — shift (act of shifting). Also: shunting
pruh — strip (long, thin piece of a bigger item). Also: stripe (a long, straight region of a single colour), lane (a lengthwise division of roadway intended for a single line of vehicles)
příčina — cause (source of an event or action).
pšenice — wheat (any member of the genus Triticum).
puška — rifle. Also: gun (any firearm resembling a rifle)
rádio — radio (radio broadcasting). Also: radio (radio receiver)
řecký — Greek (of the Greek language, people or country).
seč — fight. Also: cutting, glade, clearing
sklenice — glass (drinking vessel). Also: glass jar
smůla — pitch (sticky, gummy substance secreted by trees). Also: bad luck, resin (solid or highly viscous substance of plant or synthetic origin that is typically convertible into polymers)
solidní — solid (strong, unyielding (as of foundations etc)).
sonda — probe (a device, or part of a device, used to explore, investigate or measure). Also: probe (an investigation or inquiry)
spánek — sleep. Also: temple (region of skull)
sprej — spray (substance to be applied by dispensing). Also: sprayer (object used for spraying)
stádo — flock (of sheep). Also: herd (of various mammals)
stopa — trace. Also: foot (unit of measure), track (one section of a multitrack recording)
stupnice — scale. Also: scale (means of assigning a magnitude)
Španělsko — Spain (a country in Southern Europe, including most of the Iberian peninsula; official name: Španělské království).
špinavý — dirty (unclean; covered with or containing unpleasant substances such as dirt or grime). Also: dirty (illegal, improper)
šum — noise (sound or signal generated by random fluctuations).
tabulka — table (grid of data arranged in rows and columns). Also: table (database)
tah na branku — drive (determination in effort).
talíř — plate (a serving dish). Also: a thousand crowns (thousand units of Czech currency)
tichý — quiet (low sound). Also: still, silent (no sound)
Tokio — Tokyo (a prefecture, the capital and largest city of Japan).
totální — total. Also: complete, absolute, total
tráva — grass (plant of the family Poaceae). Also: weed (marijuana)
trpaslík — dwarf (a being from folklore). Also: dwarf (a person of short stature), dwarf (a star of relatively small size)
trubka — bugle. Also: trumpet (any pipe/wind instrument in general), pipe (hollow tube)
třetina — third (one of three equal parts of a whole).
tuhý — solid (in the state of being a solid). Also: rigid
učinit — to do. Also: to make, to render (to cause to be)
úhel — angle (figure formed by two rays).
Váhy — Libra (in astronomy). Also: the zodiacal sign Libra
veliký — great (very big, large scale). Also: great (title referring to an important leader)
vyznání — creed (that which is believed). Also: confession
zeleň — green (color). Also: greenery (green foliage or verdure)
zlom — break. Also: fault (geological fracture through rock)
zub — tooth (structure present in the mouth of many animals). Also: tooth (a projection on the edge of an instrument), tooth (projection resembling a tooth), nib, bite
žralok — shark. Also: the separation of the sole from the front of a shoe or boot (that makes the front of the shoe look like the open mouth of a shark)

Afrika — Africa (the continent south of Europe and between the Atlantic and Indian Oceans). Also: Africa (a former province of the Roman Empire, containing what is now Tunisia and portions of coastal Algeria and Libya)

Arábie — Arabia (a peninsula of West Asia between the Red Sea and the Persian Gulf; includes Jordan, Saudi Arabia, Yemen, Oman, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, and the United Arab Emirates). Also: Arabia (the region of West Asia within or adjacent to the Arabian Plate; Yemen, Oman, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, Iraq, Saudi Arabia, Jordan, the Egyptian Sinai, Palestine and the United Arab Emirates)
barel — barrel (round vessel made from staves bound with a hoop). Also: drum (barrel or large cylindrical container for liquid transport and storage)

Benátky — Venice (a port city and comune, the capital of the Metropolitan City of Venice and the region of Veneto, Italy; former capital of an independent republic). Also: Venice (a metropolitan city of Veneto, established in 2015; in full, the Metropolitan City of Venice)
Berlín — Berlin (the capital and largest city and state of Germany).
Brazílie — Brazil (a large Portuguese-speaking country in South America).
brouk — beetle. Also: sweetheart, love, honey

čestný — honest (scrupulous with regard to telling the truth; not given to swindling, lying, or fraud). Also: fair, adequate, reasonable, decent, honorary (given as an honor/honour, with no duties attached, and without payment)
číšník — waiter (male attendant who serves customers in a restaurant, cafe or similar). Also: cupbearer
čtečka — reader (any device that reads something).
čtenář — reader (person who reads a publication).
dech — breath (act or process of breathing).
doména — domain (geographic area owned or controlled by a single person or organization). Also: domain (a field or sphere of activity, influence or expertise)
drak — dragon. Also: bad and violent person (especially a woman), wyvern, kite (flying toy on string)
drát — to rip (bird feathers). Also: to jostle somewhere
dřívější — former (such that used to be but is no more). Also: earlier
Ester — Esther (biblical character). Also: the book of Esther, a female given name from Hebrew, equivalent to English Esther
evangelium — gospel (an account of the life, death, resurrection, and teachings of Jesus).
faktor — factor (one of the elements, circumstances, or influences which contribute to produce a result).
filtr — filter (device for separating impurities from a fluid or other substance). Also: filter (electronics or software to separate unwanted signal)
fráze — phrase (short written or spoken expression).
fúze — fusion (the merging of similar or different elements into a union). Also: nuclear fusion, fusion, merger (legal union of two or more corporations into a single entity)
garáž — garage (building, or section of a building, used to store a car, tools and other miscellaneous items).
hajzl — toilet (enclosed cubicle containing a water closet).
hladký — smooth (having a texture that lacks friction). Also: smooth (without difficulty, problems, or unexpected consequences or incidents)
hovado — cattle. Also: stupid person, horsefly, for example, of species Tabanus autumnalis, syn. Tabanus bovinus
chod — course (part of a meal). Also: gear, operation (the act or process of operating, of running)
chytrý — clever (mentally sharp or bright). Also: smart (technology)
indický — Indian (of or relating to India).
jádro — kernel. Also: nucleus, core (one of severals parts in a computer processor)
jaksi — rather. Also: in a way
jednotný — united. Also: uniform, singular (denoting only one of something)
Kanada — Canada (a country in North America).
kapesní — pocket (of a size suitable for putting into a pocket).
každoroční — annual (happening / occurring once a year).
kopat — to dig. Also: to kick
kotě — kitten (young cat). Also: baby, a pretty girl
králík — rabbit. Also: rabbit fur, diminutive of král

Krym — Crimea (a geographic region and peninsula in Eastern Europe, jutting out into the Black Sea; de facto occupied and annexed in 2014 as a republic of Russia, but internationally recognized as an autonomous republic of Ukraine :).
Kypr — Cyprus (an island and country in the Mediterranean Sea, normally considered politically part of Europe but geographically part of West Asia).
láhev — bottle (for liquids). Also: bottle (with rubber nipple for infants)
legie — legion (military organization in ancient Rome). Also: legion (a military unit, often composed of volunteers), legion (a multitude)
letět — to fly. Also: to be in fashion
lítost — regret. Also: remorse, repentance (a feeling of regret or remorse for doing wrong)
Lotyšsko — Latvia (a country in northeastern Europe).
manuální — manual (done or operated by hand). Also: manual (human initiated or controlled)
matice — matrix. Also: nut (of a bolt)
Mexiko — Mexico (a country in North America). Also: Mexico City (the capital city of Mexico)
minuta — minute (unit of time). Also: minute (unit of angular measure)
Mnichov — Munich (the capital and largest city of Bavaria, Germany). Also: Munich Agreement
mor — plague (specific disease). Also: pestilence, plague (any highly contagious disease)
motiv — motive (incentive to act). Also: motif
můstek — bridge. Also: diminutive of most
narazit — to hit. Also: to chance (to discover by chance), to bump into, to tap

Neapol — Naples (a port city and comune, the capital of the Metropolitan City of Naples and the region of Campania, Italy). Also: Naples (a metropolitan city of Campania, established in 2015; in full, the Metropolitan City of Naples)
někdejší — former (such that used to be but is no more).
nevolnost — nausea (feeling of physical unwellness, usually with the desire to vomit).
Norsko — Norway (a country in Scandinavia in Northern Europe; capital and largest city: Oslo).
obojek — collar (band or chain around an animal's neck).
padnout — to fit (of clothing). Also: to fall (to die, especially in battle)
pepř — pepper, a plant of the family Piperaceae. Also: pepper, a spice prepared from the fermented, dried, unripe berries of this plant
plátno — canvas (type of coarse cloth). Also: canvas (piece of cloth on which you paint), linen (material)
pobočka — branch (office of an organization with several locations).
poctivě — fairly (in a fair manner). Also: honestly (in an honest manner)
počátek — origin (of a coordinate system). Also: beginning
pojistka — safety (on a weapon). Also: fuse (device preventing the overloading of an electrical circuit), circuit breaker, policy (contract of insurance)
pramen — spring (where water emerges from the ground). Also: source, strand, ply, wisp
prášek — powder. Also: pill (small portion of a drug or drugs to be taken orally)
prostřední — middle (being in the middle or in-between).
prostý — simple. Also: free of (followed by the genitive case), injective
provozovat — to run; to operate (to control or manage, be in charge of). Also: to practice (the ongoing pursuit of a craft or profession)
přísný — strict (governed or governing by exact rules). Also: severe
přistávací dráha — runway (section for landing or take-off).
Rak — Cancer (constellation). Also: Cancer (astrological sign), a person born with the astrological sign of Cancer
rekordní — record (of or pertaing to the most extreme known value of something).
sedlo — saddle (a seat on the back of an animal). Also: saddle (a seat on a bicycle or motorcycle), saddle (a ridge, in the shape of a saddle, between two hills)
sektor — sector. Also: sector (fixed-sized unit of sequential data stored on a track of a digital medium)
sériový — serial (having to do with or arranged in a series).
sestřenice — cousin, female first cousin (daughter of a person's uncle or aunt).
sezóna — season (part of year with something special).

skokan — jumper (Someone or something that jumps, e.g. a participant in a jumping event in track or skiing.). Also: jumper (A person who attempts suicide by jumping from a great height.), Frogs of several genera:, Rana, Pelophylax, Lithobates, Staurois, Amolops, Amietia, Ptychadena
slovní spojení — phrase (single unit in the syntax of a sentence).
socha — statue. Also: sculpture (work of art)
společenský — social (outgoing). Also: social (related to society)
Srbsko — Serbia (a country on the Balkan Peninsula in Southeast Europe).
stín — shadow (dark image projected onto a surface). Also: shade (darkness where light, particularly sunlight, is blocked)
stříbrný — silver (made from silver). Also: silvery, silver (having a color like silver)

štít — shield (broad piece of protective armour). Also: shield, peak (the top, or one of the tops, of a hill, mountain or range, ending in a point), sign (identifying a shop or other establishment), gable
Švédsko — Sweden (a country in Scandinavia in Northern Europe).
terč — target (a thing to shoot at).
Tchaj-wan — Taiwan (an island and country in East Asia; capital: Taipei).
trefit — to hit (to manage to touch in the right place). Also: to find one's way
trosky — debris (rubble, wreckage, scattered remains of something destroyed).
Turecko — Turkey (a country located in Eastern Thrace in Southeastern Europe and Anatolia in West Asia).
útěk — flight (act of fleeing). Also: escape
váha — weight. Also: scale, scales, weighing scale
Varšava — Warsaw (the capital city of Poland).
vlákno — thread. Also: filament (of a light bulb), fibre
vrba — willow (tree or shrub). Also: good confidant
vypínač — switch (device to turn electric current on and off).

zápis — minute (of a meeting). Also: memorandum, memo, note, registration, enrollment (into a school or a university exam), notation (the act, process, method, or an instance of representing by a system or set of marks, signs, figures, or characters), notation (a system of characters, symbols, or abbreviated expressions used in an art or science or in mathematics or logic to express technical facts or quantities)
zasáhnout — to hit. Also: to intervene
zavřený — closed (of door). Also: close (of a vowel)
žalovat — to sue. Also: to tell on somebody
adresa — address (direction for letters). Also: address
ateliér — atelier (workshop or studio, especially for an artist, designer or fashion house). Also: atelier (film studio)
atlet — athlete (participant in track and field sports).
aukce — auction (public event where goods or property are sold to the highest bidder).
balit — to pack. Also: to wrap, to pick women for a date
balón — balloon (toy). Also: balloon, aerostat (transport vehicle)
bašta — bastion. Also: hut on a pond dam
Belgie — Belgium (a country in Western Europe that has borders with the Netherlands, Germany, Luxembourg and France).
Bělorusko — Belarus (a country in Eastern Europe; official name: Běloruská republika).

bojovník — fighter (person who fights, a combatant). Also: martial artist (practitioner and/or preceptor of martial arts), warrior (person who is actively engaged in battle, conflict or warfare; a soldier)
bombardování — bombing (dropping bombs from the air).
bubeník — drummer (one who plays the drums).
čerpat — to pump (to use a pump to move liquid or gas).
činit — to make (to cause to be). Also: to tan (to change an animal hide into leather by soaking it in tannic acid)
čtvrť — quarter (section of a town). Also: district
Dánsko — Denmark (a country in Northern Europe).
děj — process (path of succession of states through which a system passes). Also: plot, story (in a movie, theater play, book of fiction)
dějepis — history (aggregate of past events). Also: history (branch of knowledge that studies the past)
Drážďany — Dresden (the capital city of Saxony, Germany).
duha — rainbow (optical phenomenon). Also: stave (narrow strip of wood)
důchod — pension (retirement benefit). Also: income (money earned)
dvouhra — singles (a game between individual players).
epizoda — episode (one part of a media series).
Estonsko — Estonia (a country in northeastern Europe, on the southeastern coast of the Baltic Sea).
Finsko — Finland (a country in Northern Europe).
frakce — faction (group of people). Also: fraction
granát — grenade (small explosive device, designed to be thrown by hand or launched from a grenade launcher). Also: garnet
Gruzie — Georgia (a transcontinental country in the Caucasus region of Europe and Asia, on the coast of the Black Sea, often considered to belong politically to Europe).
hlavice — capital (uppermost part of a column). Also: caput, capitulum, part of the hilt of a sword, warhead
Holandsko — Holland (a traditional region of the Netherlands formed by two modern provinces, North Holland and South Holland). Also: Holland, the Netherlands (a country in Western Europe)
hořet — to burn. Also: to feel (love), to want, to be aflame
hrabat — to rake. Also: to poke, fumble, root about
choroba — disease; sickness, illness (abnormal health condition).
Chorvatsko — Croatia (a country on the Balkan Peninsula in Southeast Europe).
impuls síly — impulse (in physics, the integral of force over time).
Irsko — Ireland (an island and country in northwestern Europe).
islámský — Islamic (of, pertaining to, originating in, characteristic of, or deriving from Islam).
Island — Iceland (an island and country in the North Atlantic Ocean in Europe).
jakkoli — however. Also: in any way
jako prase — as hell (to a great extent or degree).
jako svině — as hell (to a great extent or degree).
jmění — fortune (lots of riches). Also: verbal noun of mít
kapitál — capital (the means to acquire goods and services, including financial means). Also: capital (already-produced durable goods available for production)
Kapitol — Capitol (the building in Washington, D.C., where the Congress of the United States meets).
koncertní — concert (musical entertainment in which several voices or instruments take part).
kontext — context (text in which a word appears).
krajní — extreme (in the greatest or highest degree; intense).
krém — custard. Also: cream (oil or fat emulsion to apply to the skin)
kresba — drawing (a picture, likeness, diagram or representation, usually drawn on paper). Also: drawing (creation of a picture)
kreslit — to draw (to produce a picture).
krmit — to feed (object: animal, child, old person).
kurátor — curator (person who manages, administers or organizes a collection). Also: curator, trustee; guardian, curate (unordained parish representative)
léčit — to heal. Also: to treat (to apply medical care to)
letecká pošta — airmail (the system of conveying mail using aircraft).
lovit — to hunt (to chase down prey).
Maďarsko — Hungary (a country in Central Europe).
malíř — painter (artist). Also: painter (one who paints surfaces using a paintbrush)
náhled — preview. Also: thumbnail (of an image), view, look, glance
nájem — tenancy. Also: rent (payment for an apartment or other equipment)
nakládat — to load (goods etc for transport). Also: to plate (food), to pickle, to treat, handle (used with the preposition s)
nakupovat — to shop; to go shopping. Also: to shop around
naložit — to load (goods etc for transport). Also: to plate (food), to pickle, to treat, handle (used with the preposition s)
naplnit — to fill (of a container like bottle). Also: to fill (become full)
nešťastný — unhappy (of a person). Also: unlucky, unfortunate
neutrální — neutral (not taking sides in a conflict such as war).
nevlastní syn — stepson (son of one's spouse and not of oneself).
Nigérie — Nigeria (a country in West Africa, south of the country of Niger).
nosič — carrier (device for carrying something). Also: support (of a function)
obhajoba — defense (argument in support or justification of something).
obsluha — service. Also: operation, operating (of a machine), staff, crew
omluva — apology (speech by which one asks for forgiveness, by which one justifies an act).
ostatky — remains (what is left after a person dies).

otočit — to turn, to rotate [with accusative ‘’] (the object is free). Also: to turn, to rotate [with instrumental ‘’] (the object is attached to something), to turn around, to make a turn, to come full circle
otroctví — slavery (condition of servitude endured by a slave). Also: slavery (condition in which one is captivated or subjugated)
pálit — to burn (to cause to be consumed by fire). Also: to fire, to shoot at, to run swiftly, to sting
párek — sausage (small, uncooked). Also: pair; diminutive of pár
pasáž — passage (paragraph or section of text or music with particular meaning).
péct — to bake. Also: to roast
pěstovat — to grow (to cause to become bigger, especially plants).
plnit — to fill. Also: to fulfill (object: promise), to fulfill (object: requirement)
podávat — to pass. Also: to serve (meal, etc), to administer (a medication)
podepsat — to sign (put one's signature). Also: to write sb's name
podvozek — chassis (base frame of a motor vehicle).
pohnout — to move. Also: to vary, range, to mingle, socialize (with a specified group)
pojistné — premium (the amount to be paid for an insurance policy).
pojmenovat — to name (to give a name to).
poštovné — postage (the charge for posting an item).
potkat — to meet, encounter [with accusative ‘’] (certainly unintentionally). Also: to meet, to encounter (likely unintentionally)
povídat — to chat (be engaged in informal conversation).
pravidelný — regular (with constant frequency). Also: regular
proměnit — to turn (change fundamentally). Also: to transform, to metamorphose, to turn, to transform

questionTemplates.questionFormat: Which word is closest in meaning to '{targetLanguageWord}'?
